fields.title: IELTS Listening Practice
fields.description: Short listening comprehension test. Read the transcript and choose the best answer.

fields.title: IELTS Reading Practice
fields.description: Read the passage and answer the questions below.

fields.title: IELTS Writing Practice
fields.description: Multiple-choice questions about academic writing structure and grammar.

fields.title: IELTS Speaking Practice
fields.description: Choose the most natural and grammatically correct response.

fields.text: Transcript: 'The lecture starts at half past nine in lecture hall B.' — When does the lecture start?
Transcript: 'You can borrow up to six books, but reference materials must stay in the library.' — How many books can a student take home?
Transcript: 'The campus tour leaves from the main entrance every Monday at 2 p.m.' — Where does the campus tour begin?
Transcript: 'Please bring a passport-sized photograph and proof of address when you register.' — Which two items are required at registration?

options.label: Passport photo and proof of address

fields.text: Transcript: 'Coffee is available all day, but the kitchen closes at seven.' — What closes at seven?
Passage: 'Bologna is widely regarded as the oldest university in continuous operation in the Western world, founded in 1088.' — According to the passage, what is Bologna known for?

options.label: Being the largest university in Europe
Being the oldest university in continuous operation

fields.text: Passage: 'Most Italian master's programmes require an IELTS score of 6.0 or higher, with some competitive courses asking for 6.5.' — What is the typical minimum IELTS requirement?
Passage: 'The Universitaly portal is the official platform used by non-EU students to pre-enrol in Italian universities.' — Who uses the Universitaly portal for pre-enrolment?
Passage: 'A Declaration of Value (DOV) is issued by the Italian consulate in the country where the diploma was obtained.' — Where is a Declaration of Value issued?

options.label: At the consulate where the diploma was obtained

fields.text: Passage: 'After receiving an acceptance letter, students must apply for a type-D study visa at least sixty days before departure.' — How early should students apply for the visa?
Choose the most formal opening for an academic essay introduction.
Which sentence uses the correct linking word for contrast?

options.label: It rained heavily, therefore the match was cancelled.
It rained heavily; however, the match continued as planned.
It rained heavily, because the match was cancelled.
It rained heavily, so the match was cancelled.

fields.text: Pick the sentence with correct subject–verb agreement.

options.label: The list of items are on the desk.
The list of items is on the desk.
The list of items were on the desk.
The list of items be on the desk.

fields.text: Which option best paraphrases: 'The number of international students has risen significantly over the past decade.'

options.label: More foreign students have arrived in the last ten years.
Over the previous ten years, the international student population has grown considerably.

fields.text: Which sentence is the strongest thesis statement for an opinion essay?

options.label: Online learning is something many people use.
Although online learning offers flexibility, it cannot fully replace in-person instruction.
I will write about online learning.

fields.text: Examiner: 'Can you describe the place where you grew up?' Choose the most natural opening.

options.label: Sure. I grew up in a small coastal town in the south of my country.
Place where grow up me is small.

fields.text: Examiner: 'Do you prefer to study alone or in a group? Why?' Choose the best answer.

options.label: I prefer studying alone since I can concentrate better, although group sessions are useful before exams.

fields.text: Which phrase is most appropriate to politely disagree?

options.label: I see your point, but I'd argue that...

fields.text: Choose the sentence that best uses a linking phrase to extend an answer.

options.label: I like reading. In addition, it helps me unwind after a long day.

fields.text: Examiner: 'How has technology changed the way young people communicate?' Pick the best response.

options.label: Young people now rely heavily on instant messaging and social media, which makes communication faster but often more superficial.
